description: Diagnostic decision tree for A2485_EVTc (MacBook Pro 16" M1 Pro/Max). Follow sequentially from Step 1.
WARNING: This is the EVTc variant (820-02382). Page numbers differ from EVTs (820-02100). PP3V8_AON is 42A design. 5 Monaco instances. 3 Viper power stages. USB-C 5V uses UF800 (LT8640) left and UG800 (TPS62180) right -- NOT load switches.

eason_reference.source: M1 series standby circuit and power-on principle, and 20V boost circuit (Eason @ iphone_cpu_fix)

forced_start_bypass.description: Bypass protective isolation. On Pro/Max, comparator is a summing amplifier with BOM-selectable thresholds on AON throttle page 60.
steps: 1. Connect PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN to 3.8V externally (4.3V with display)
2. Force comparator output high at summing amp output (page 60) to assert PMU_VDD_HI
3. Press start button OR connect charger for 3 seconds

interpretation.forced_start_works: FRONT END fault — CD3217, charger IC, fuses, MagSafe/Whamola path, boost circuit
interpretation.forced_start_fails: BACK END fault — PMU, SoC, clocks, ROM, NAND

p3v8aon_fault_l.description: Active-low fault flag from SVR AON controller U5700. If LOW, SVR has detected overcurrent/overvoltage/thermal fault — the SVR itself has a problem. If HIGH, SVR is healthy, look downstream. 42A design runs hotter than A2337/A2338.
p3v8aon_fault_l.action: Single probe eliminates entire SVR AON section

pmu_active_ready.description: Goes HIGH only after MPMU (U8100) completes full startup sequence. If PP3V8_AON present but PMU_ACTIVE_READY stays low, fault is inside PMU, clocks, ROM, or SoC handshake.
pmu_active_ready.action: Skips past power section to compute section

crystal_32khz.description: PMU_REFCLK32K_OUT — MPMU cannot start without this clock. No oscillation = dead crystal = board won't start regardless of power path health.
crystal_32khz.action: 1 second with oscilloscope eliminates entire clock failure category

soc_force_dfu.description: Forces DFU mode bypassing macOS boot. If DFU works but board won't boot normally, entire power delivery and SoC hardware is fine — problem is NAND/firmware.
soc_force_dfu.action: Instantly separates hardware from software faults. No disassembly needed.

phase_balance.description: Individual phase current sense resistors for the 3-phase SVR AON (42A design). Measure voltage drop across each while running. Zero on one while others are equal = dead phase. Summing amp (page 60) aggregates all three — check individual sense resistors to isolate.
phase_balance.action: Identifies exact dead phase without removing anything

viper_fault.description: Active-low fault from Viper VR (U9300) which powers PCPU domains. If LOW, Viper has detected fault in one of its 3 power stages. Check stages individually (pages 94-96). Max variant: Stage 2 powers PCPU1.
viper_fault.action: Single probe eliminates entire Viper/PCPU power delivery section

monaco_fault.description: Monaco VR alert signals from GPU/Fabric voltage regulators. 5 Monaco instances (UA100-UA500). Check GPIO connections on each instance. Max variant: Monaco 3 (UA400) and 4 (UA500) are Max-specific additions.
monaco_fault.action: Identifies which Monaco instance is faulting without probing all 5

magsafe_detect.description: MagSafe detection from U5500 UPC5 controller (page 55). If MagSafe connected but no charge, check J5401 connector (page 54) and U5500 controller. A2485 has MagSafe AND USB-C charging paths.
magsafe_detect.action: Separates MagSafe path from USB-C charging path

cd3217_sys_detect.description: Goes high when charger IC recognizes valid power source. 5V at USB-C but SYS_DETECT low = CD3217/CD3218 fault. Check UF400 (page 74), UF500 (page 76), UG400 (page 83).
cd3217_sys_detect.action: Splits entire input path in one probe

hdmi_power.description: HDMI subsystem power via Madea UK700 (page 91). If HDMI not working, check UH901 3.3V LDO (page 93) and UH001 1.2V VR (page 92). A2485-specific peripheral.
hdmi_power.action: Isolates HDMI subsystem from main board

sd_card_power.description: SD card reader power via UK500 GL9755 (page 94) and UK830 TPS62135 3.3V (page 97). A2485-specific peripheral.
sd_card_power.action: Isolates SD card subsystem from main board

check_sequence: Check PPDCIN_AON (charger input, page 52)
Check SYS_DETECT signal from battery connector (U5150/Q5155, page 51)
Check CD3217/CD3218 port controllers (UF400 page 74, UF500 page 76, UG400 page 83)
Check charger IC U5200 area gate drives G1-G6 (page 52)
Check dual inductors LS230/LS231 (page 52)
Check MagSafe path: U5500 UPC5 (page 55), J5401 connector (page 54)
Thermal cam on charger area for shorted FETs
Check P3V8AON_PWR_EN (derived from SYS_DETECT, page 57)
Check P3V8AON_FAULT_L (active low fault, page 57)
Check 3-phase output and ISENSE summing amp (page 60)
Check phase 1 VSENSE gain 0.7%, phase 2/3 VSENSE gain 2%, 4:1 weight for phase 1 ATREE (page 60)
Thermal cam on U5700 and phase drivers
Check nominal thresholds BOM table (page 60): BOM1 0V=3.6/1.20, 3V8=32.7/1.20
Check P5V2TPS_PWR_EN (page 122)
Check PPVZN_P5VS2TPS input
Verify SET ONE OPTION FOR PBUS CAPS selected (PACK_OPTION=5VS2TPS_PBUS-B12/D2/D12)
Startup time 4.7/7.5/10.5ms -- may need to wait
Check PP3V8_AON_MPMU (ISENSE branch to U8100)
Check PPBUS_AON_MPMU (via XWB170, page 81)
Check PMU_ACTIVE_READY (page 83)
Check PMU_FAULT_INT_L (page 83)
Check MPMU BUCK output caps (page 80 left side: MASTER columns 0-10)
Thermal cam on U8100

tree.probe: SPMU output caps area (page 80 right side: SLAVE columns)

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON_SPMU (ISENSE branch to U7700)
Check SPMU_READY / SPMU_INDF (page 79)
Check SPMU BUCK output caps (page 80 right side)
Check SPMU_RESET_1N (page 79)
Note: BUCK15 is NC on sC designs (page 78)
Check PVDDVDN_FAULT_L (page 93)
Check PVDDVDN_BOOT1 signal (page 93)
Check 5 phase input cap banks PH1-PH5 (Viper support, page 92)
Check all 3 power stages: Stage 1 (page 94), Stage 2 (page 95), Stage 3 (page 96)
Check PP1V8_S1_MON_REG (Viper monitoring, page 96)
Thermal cam on Viper controller U9300 and power stage MOSFETs
Max variant: Stage 2 powers PCPU1 -- check if only Max-specific stage fails

tree.yes: SoC domains powered. Fault is downstream. Go to Step 8.

check_sequence: Check which Monaco instance is failing (UA100-UA500, pages 101-105)
Check CLVR GPIO connections on each instance for ALERT signals
Check CLVR fabric throttle UC000 (74AP1G06, page 120)
Check Monaco input caps (11uF APN 138500328 on each instance)
Max variant: Monaco 3 (UA400) and 4 (UA500) are Max-specific additions
Check Reference Design Overrides: Monaco 3 and 4 need MAKE_BASE fix

substeps.probe: Display power sequencer UP710 (page 113), Luxe backlight UP800 (page 114)

substeps.note: EVTc uses dedicated buck regulators, NOT load switches

substeps.probe: Madea UK700 (page 91), UH901 3.3V LDO (page 93), UH001 1.2V VR (page 92)
